decisions.decision: Use multi-window Tauri architecture (pet-window + panel-window) instead of single window
alternatives: Single window with CSS pointer-events switching
Single window with dynamic XShape input regions
decisions.rationale: Pet window requires click-through + transparent + always-on-top. Panel window needs normal mouse interaction. These are mutually exclusive X11 window properties that cannot coexist in one window without complex, fragile XShape synchronization.
decisions.impact: Two Tauri WebView processes. ~5MB extra memory. Inter-window communication via Tauri events.

decisions.decision: Store timestamps as INTEGER (Unix epoch seconds) not TEXT (ISO 8601)
decisions.rationale: Integer comparison is faster for time-range queries (compression age checks). Most compact storage.
decisions.impact: Frontend must convert to display format.

decisions.decision: Use Git CLI subprocess instead of git2/libgit2 binding
decisions.rationale: Git CLI guaranteed present (context dependency). Avoids 3MB+ binary size. Only need simple operations (log, remote, status).
decisions.impact: ~10ms subprocess overhead per call. Acceptable given 30s minimum interval.

decisions.decision: Brain as single module with internal structs rather than 4 separate modules
alternatives: Separate context, status, distillation, compression modules
decisions.rationale: Tight state coupling. Splitting increases messaging overhead without benefit for a small app.
decisions.impact: Larger single module (~800 LOC) but simpler dependency graph.

decisions.decision: UUID v7 (time-ordered) for all primary keys
decisions.rationale: Time-sortable, benefits timeline queries and SQLite B-tree locality.
decisions.impact: Requires uuid crate with v7 feature.
